title: First Credit Checkpoint
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you use credit as a tool for financial growth rather than a source of debt?
What strategies will help you maintain responsible credit habits over time?

stages.prompt: Before applying for your first credit card, what should be your primary consideration?

options.label: Getting a card for social status
options.reflection: Using credit cards for status can lead to overspending and debt. Credit should be a financial tool, not a status symbol.

options.label: Understanding your ability to repay
options.reflection: Exactly! Responsible credit use starts with knowing your limits and ensuring you can make payments on time.

options.label: Maximizing reward points and benefits
options.reflection: While rewards are nice, they shouldn't be the primary focus. Responsible repayment should always come first.

options.label: Getting approved for the highest limit
options.reflection: Higher limits can be tempting but dangerous if you're not ready to handle them responsibly.

stages.prompt: What's the most important habit to develop with your first credit card?

options.label: Paying the full balance every month
options.reflection: Perfect! This is the golden rule of credit card use. It builds good credit while avoiding interest charges.

options.label: Using it for all purchases to build credit
options.reflection: Using credit for everything can lead to overspending. It's better to use it strategically for purchases you can afford.

options.label: Paying only the minimum amount due
options.reflection: Minimum payments lead to accumulating interest and debt. Always aim to pay the full balance.

options.label: Making purchases right before the statement date
options.reflection: Timing purchases isn't as important as making responsible choices and paying on time.

stages.prompt: How should you handle credit card offers and promotions?

options.label: Accept all offers to maximize benefits
options.reflection: Accepting too many offers can lead to overspending and financial complexity. Choose wisely based on your needs.

options.label: Ignore all credit offers completely
options.reflection: While you shouldn't accept every offer, completely ignoring credit can limit your financial tools and opportunities.

options.label: Only compare interest rates, ignore other terms
options.reflection: Interest rates are important, but fees, rewards, and terms also matter. Consider the complete package.

options.label: Evaluate each offer carefully based on your needs
options.reflection: Exactly! Good financial decisions come from careful evaluation, not impulsive acceptance of every offer.

stages.prompt: What should you do if you can't make a full payment on your credit card?

options.label: Skip the payment and hope for the best
options.reflection: Skipping payments damages your credit score and leads to late fees. Always make at least the minimum payment.

options.label: Borrow from another source to pay it off
options.reflection: Borrowing to pay credit cards can create a cycle of debt. It's better to reduce spending and pay what you can.

options.label: Pay at least the minimum and contact your issuer
options.reflection: Good! Making minimum payments protects your credit while you work on paying the full amount. Communication with your issuer is also key.

options.label: Delay payment until next month when you have more money
options.reflection: Delaying payments leads to late fees and credit damage. It's always better to pay something rather than nothing.

stages.prompt: What's the best approach to building a good credit history?

options.label: Open multiple credit accounts quickly
options.reflection: Opening too many accounts at once can hurt your credit score and make it harder to manage your finances.

options.label: Use credit consistently and responsibly over time
options.reflection: Perfect! Good credit history is built through consistent, responsible use over months and years, not quick fixes.

options.label: Maintain high balances to show credit usage
options.reflection: High balances hurt your credit utilization ratio. It's better to keep balances low and pay them off regularly.

options.label: Avoid credit entirely to prevent any risk
options.reflection: While avoiding credit eliminates risk, it also prevents you from building the credit history needed for loans, mortgages, and other financial opportunities.
